revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (2).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|24.7|N|140|34|10.7|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki]] [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (9).jpg|right|thumb|270px|Signpost at Torazuka Kofun showing interior]] The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline}} * [https://www.ibarakiguide.jp/events/events-81546.html Ibaraki Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.hitachinaka.lg.jp/kanko_site/4/2/6451.html Hitachinaka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/kuni/shiseki/12-16/12-16.html Ibaraki Prefectural Board of Education] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Authority control}}
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hitachinaka, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]

==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (2).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|24.7|N|140|34|10.7|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki]] [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (9).jpg|right|thumb|270px|Signpost at Torazuka Kofun showing interior]] The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].

It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].

It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (2).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|24.7|N|140|34|10.7|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki]] [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (9).jpg|right|thumb|270px|Signpost at Torazuka Kofun showing interior]] The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (2).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|24.7|N|140|34|10.7|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki]] [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (9).jpg|right|thumb|270px|Signpost at Torazuka Kofun showing interior]] The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (2).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|24.7|N|140|34|10.7|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki]] [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (9).jpg|right|thumb|270px|Signpost at Torazuka Kofun showing interior]] The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} * [https://www.ibarakiguide.jp/events/events-81546.html Ibaraki Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.hitachinaka.lg.jp/kanko_site/4/2/6451.html Hitachinaka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/kuni/shiseki/12-16/12-16.html Ibaraki Prefectural Board of Education] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Authority control}}

The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (2).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|24.7|N|140|34|10.7|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki]] [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[file:虎塚古墳 2009.03.29 - panoramio (9).jpg|right|thumb|270px|Signpost at Torazuka Kofun showing interior]] The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[Katsuta Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[Nakane Station]] on the [[Minato Line]].

It was excavated by [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Torazuka Kofun]][[en:Torazuka Kofun]][[ja:虎塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11625282]] Wikidata name: Torazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Torazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.373528|N|140.569639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nakane|WD=Q97160643}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[:en:Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)]]

It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[:en:Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].

[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[:en:Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].

It was excavated by [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Torazuka Kofun]][[en:Torazuka Kofun]][[ja:虎塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Torazuka Kofun]][[en:Torazuka Kofun]][[ja:虎塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Torazuka Kofun]][[en:Torazuka Kofun]][[ja:虎塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11625282]] Wikidata name: Torazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Torazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.373528|N|140.569639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nakane|WD=Q97160643}} | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of [[:en:Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|Hitachinaka]] in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Katsuta Station|Katsuta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from [[:en:Nakane Station|Nakane Station]] on the [[:en:Minato Line|Minato Line]].
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)]]

The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Katsuta Station|en|Katsuta Station|WD=Q859695|lt=Katsuta Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from {{ill|Nakane Station|en|Nakane Station|WD=Q6960259|lt=Nakane Station}} on the {{ill|Minato Line|en|Minato Line|WD=Q6863245|lt=Minato Line}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of {{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|WD=Q696772|lt=Hitachinaka}} in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Torazuka Kofun]][[en:Torazuka Kofun]][[ja:虎塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Torazuka Kofun]][[en:Torazuka Kofun]][[ja:虎塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Torazuka Kofun]][[en:Torazuka Kofun]][[ja:虎塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Torazuka Kofun | native_name =虎塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Torazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Torazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.373528|N|140.569639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nakane|WD=Q97160643}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of {{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|WD=Q696772|lt=Hitachinaka}} in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Torazuka Kofun is located in a forested plateau at an elevation of about 20 meters, where several other ''kofun'' tumuli have been found. It was excavated by {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|WD=Q46492|lt=Meiji University}} in 1973, and was found to be a rare example of a [[decorated kofun]]. The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Katsuta Station|en|Katsuta Station|WD=Q859695|lt=Katsuta Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from {{ill|Nakane Station|en|Nakane Station|WD=Q6960259|lt=Nakane Station}} on the {{ill|Minato Line|en|Minato Line|WD=Q6863245|lt=Minato Line}}.
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|WD=Q16248240|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline}} * [https://www.ibarakiguide.jp/events/events-81546.html Ibaraki Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.hitachinaka.lg.jp/kanko_site/4/2/6451.html Hitachinaka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/kuni/shiseki/12-16/12-16.html Ibaraki Prefectural Board of Education] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Authority control}}

The tumulus is a medium-sized {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 56.5 meters. The tumulus was once surrounded by a moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Internally, the tumulus has a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made from large blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} that opens to the south side of the posterior circular portion, and which was found to be intact from ancient times. The chamber has a length of 2.8 meters, width of 1.8 meters, height of 1.4 meters. Within was the skeletal remains of one adult male. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a lacquered small iron sword, iron spears, fragments of armor and horse harnesses, iron stirrups, and other items. Of great interest was the fact that the walls, floor and ceiling of the burial chamber had been painted and decorated. The floor and ceiling were vermillion, whereas the walls were coating in a white clay and covered with geometric patterns in vermillion. These drawings included concentric circles, hourglass-shaped designed and a serrated border at the ceiling.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The opening of the burial chamber was the first time in Japan that a scientific survey (for temperature and humidity, microorganisms, etc.) had been conducted prior to the opening of the intact stone chamber. The construction of the tumulus is estimated to be in the first half of the 7th century.
The tumulus is open to the public for a couple of days each year in the spring March–April and autumn November. It is located about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Katsuta Station|en|Katsuta Station|WD=Q859695|lt=Katsuta Station|1=Katsuta Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Or about five minutes by car from {{ill|Nakane Station|en|Nakane Station|WD=Q6960259|lt=Nakane Station|1=Nakane Station}} on the {{ill|Minato Line|en|Minato Line|WD=Q6863245|lt=Minato Line|1=Minato Line}}.
;Total length: 56.5 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 38.5 meters wide x 7.2 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 32.5 meter diameter x 7.5 meters

The {{nihongo|'''Torazuka Kofun'''|虎塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in what is now the Nakane neighborhood of city of {{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|WD=Q696772|lt=Hitachinaka|1=Hitachinaka, Ibaraki}} in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1974.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209690 |title=虎塚古墳|trans-title= Torazuka Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
